claim: DeepSeek's OCR is fundamentally about context optical compression: storing 1000 text tokens in ~100 vision tokens with 97% accuracy (10x compression)
actionable: Use vision-based compression to extend context windows; render long text histories as images and decode back to improve token efficiency

claim: DeepSeek's deep encoder uses a two-stage approach: SAM model (18M params) for high-res attention + CNN compression (16x) + CLIP for global attention
actionable: Apply multi-stage vision encoding (SAM + compression + CLIP) to efficiently process documents and achieve 800 vision tokens vs 6000 text tokens

claim: Vision tokens maintain 60% accuracy even at 20x compression (50 vision tokens for 1000 text tokens), enabling extreme context extension
actionable: Explore ultra-high compression ratios for less critical context (e.g., old conversation history); balance compression ratio with accuracy needs
